channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: STOP THE COUNT — 26 replies — 104.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:35 UTC

OP

!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62067455
>>62067445
Why? LINK was above $9.00000 again so they dumped BTC

Has happened like 10 times now

62067479
>>62067445
the cuckolds of crypto

62067508
>>62067445
>crypto currency is linked inversely to a war in the middle east
no one would believe this in 2015

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62067615
https://youtube.com/watch?v=_asNhzXq72w

62067941
>>62067445
I've just bought 300 LINK

62068067
>>62067445
It’s going to $5 this year.
Buckle up, buttercup.

62068078
>>62068067
You are making me fearful, uncertain and doubtful about my investment decision, should i sell?

62068102
>>62068078
We all know you’ll hold it to zero.
Treat yourself and buy more while they are cheap.

62068733
>>62067445
ngl really though I would have made it from LINK by now, pretty disappointing. I also voted for Trump exclusively to help pump markets and he fucked everything up instead, last time I single issue vote, back to being a demsucker libtard for me.

62069075
>>62068733
idk dude reps are ugly and fat and they larp like they are le based aryans. dems atleast dont do shit and stay out of our way i have to hear about retard fat fucking trump every day, literal retards pouring in while we are invading iran like nigga.

62069093
>>62067455
It's like 2022-2023 all over again

62069095
>>62069075
>dems atleast dont do shit and stay out of our way
unfortunately not the case, and especially so now that idiots in this industry sucked up to trump. I think crypto is fucked either way, one side just wants to rob the industry while dangling empty promises and the other side just wants to strangle it with red tape

62069115
>>62068067
Thanks god I need to slurp more!
And if it could go back to the $1 to $2 range it would be great.
Excited desu.

62069829
>>62067445
Any oldfag here have the "2 dollar stable coin" whitepaper shitpost?
That's where we're headed

62069973
>>62069829
Smart money will buy everything they can at $5

62069997
>>62067445
Imagine stll being a LINK bagholder.
Is this 2018??

62070008
>>62069093
nah, your shitcoin is going back to $2 cuckold

62074235
>>62067445
Didn't this useless scam hit $20, why are Linkies such brainwashed cucks holding?

62075993
>>62067445
Adem won.. THE cuckolds of crypto lost!
I kneel to chADEM

62077301
>>62067445
the number keeps going up

62077313
>>62067455
And every time they do it's a gift to you to buy

62077316
>>62068067
>5

You are a deluded cuck linky, it's going to $3!

62077319
>>62069075
>>62068733
Yeah guys I agree, let's all keep voting for different colours, our vote counts and makes a huge difference to what happens in our country ;)

62079168
>>62067445
Is now a good time to buy?

62079210
I like chainlink token

62079244
>>62079168

Especially if you want to join the ranks of the baggies

item.title: *inhales* — 37 replies — 83.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
*inhales*

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:19 UTC

OP

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA OH LINKIES

Early replies (first 3)

62070715
Why would this be a bad thing for link?

62070725
>>62070715
well let me put it this way:
why would link EVER PUMP EVER AGAIN!!??!?!?!
fuckin stupid ass stinkbag

62070738
why did you crop out the full post?
>I'm not retiring. I saw something.
>I'm leaving to join a team of scientists and entrepreneurs building at the intersection of Frontier AI, scientific discovery and institutional finance. Researchers from Oxford, Harvard and Cambridge. Engineers from SpaceX, Google and Uber.
>More to come. But the reason I left the best job I've ever had is simple: this is what comes next and I had to join the team building it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62070843
>>62070819
good. we want you to suffer, stinkbag

62070876
>>62070843
I noticed.

62071007
>>62070876
how does it feel knowing your net worth will soon be negative, thanks to stink dumping :}

62071018
>>62071007
>negative net worth
How does it feel to be a double digit iq african?

62071025
>>62071018
>retard doesn't know how negatives work
>accuses others of being stupid
poetry :}

62071053
>>62071018
It’s absolutely possible to have negative net worth lmao

62071058
>>62070725
Jesus, what an embarrassing spaghetti spill. The absolute state of you.

62071080
>>62071058
>dude, memes from 90s rap songs
cringe :}

62071105
>>62070698
Thanks for keeping us updated on SWIFTs executives fellow anon

62071131
>>62070698
He should've just given Ripple a fair pass and he wouldn't have gotten shitcanned.

62071230
>>62070698
its funny how anyone who belibved in link abandons it sooner or later. hahaah

link was such a massive dissapointment.

i feel bad for all the loyal supporters of it.

62071340
Oh my

62071417
>>62071340
What does that even mean. Does this mean swift will be using xrp lol

62071423
>>62070715
you cant replace people, they said

62071524
>>62071417
they don't even know what certified means

62071543
>>62071423
Who said that? No one said that

62071620
>>62070698
It’s so over

62071927
>>62071620
For stinkies

62072238
>>62071417
In tumorweeks, evwrything can magically happen what will pump your centralized shittokens.

62072254
>>62071018
>avg cuckold HR lady vacation financier stinkie

62073502
so Kemal leaves chainlink to work on an ai project and then 2 years later tom leaves swift to work on ai project that " has Researchers from Oxford, Harvard and Cambridge. Engineers from SpaceX, Google and Uber." and i'm supposed to be nervous and sell my link? yea in your wildest dreams. NEVER SELLING. POOL IS CLOSED.

62073562
>>62071007
you're german and hold link

62073601
>>62070738
>why did you crop out the full post?
>>I'm not retiring. I saw something.

aka

>duuuuuuuuuuuuude he's still in on LE STHHHHHHECRET


HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAA

the denial is strong with you boyscouts. but that's because you are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62073623
prophetic words here from anon last fall


the cult takes L after L
the spin job gets harder and harder each time


your hubris was your downfall

62073722
>>62070698
He's going to his bunker before shkt hits the fan

62074263
>>62070698

he won't have conflict of interest

62074346
>>62074263
eh fair enough, either he got canned or left to go all in before the convergence

62076104

does he read our pasta's?

62077387

They bought GTreasury which did that, and now renamed it.
Its like buying a mcdonalds, renaming it burgerking and now bragging about how burgerking did 15 million in sales last year lmao

item.title: Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:31 UTC

OP

Did you guys see that just recently HEX became the 3rd most held token on all of Ethereum?
Only behind USDC and Tether

Does this give it more legitimacy or will most see through this mass marketing dust campaign?
Either way, it's not cheap to send your coin to 5 million people in a shill campaign.....

Early replies (first 3)

62077138
>>62077135
its totally legit saar, ape in lifesavings!

62077151
read pic
I bca finding out which "wrong-talk" did I type that our janitor is malding over

62077172
>>62077151
Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up, so if you're going to fork it anyways, why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?

I agree there is a lot of hate-marketing out there giving bad optics, so many twitter accounts hating and shitting on RH and his coins daily and you just know it's all fueled by rage over their bags they're down bad on

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077902
fuck off heart you gangster fuck

62077984
>>62077902
how is it so hard to find him? hes literally in estonia and probably hits up the luxury stores once a week lol

62078130
>>62077984
When X enabled location info for profiles last year his account showed south east asia.

>>62077902
fake news, all assault charges have been dropped
it was quite possibly completely made up just to get him on the interpol list since you can't be on there for non-violent crimes.

62078173
>>62077135
What’s the difference between pHEX, eHEX and HEX in ethereum? I can’t tell you because idgaf I’m all in pDAI niggers

62078189
>>62077172
>why not make a new chain where the ETH FagFoundation can't make another update that fucks it all up with higher fees?
because you're overcomplicating the project which was supposed to be pure game-theory of stakers vs LPers/ traders

now you have a shitshow that sent this to a -99% death chart
it's dead because of it

>Airdropping a fresh coin would mess everything up
it would mess NOTHING up
hex was an airdrop to bitcoin holders in the first place

62078192
>>62078173
see
this is what I mean
now you have the pDAI shitshow
you have pHEX, eHEX, pulseX
it's a fucking circus

richard fucking failed because he didn't listen to his own advice

62078714
>>62078130
sure thing mr goldstein

62078715
>>62078130
>non-violent
rape is a violent crime

62078740
>>62078173
pDAI to Dorra.

62078749
>>62077135
>3rd most wash-traded coin on ethereum
Ftfya

item.title: Renting 38% of net pay in VHCOL — 18 replies — 41.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Renting 38% of net pay in VHCOL

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:20 UTC

OP

Got a job paying ~12k net in VHCOL city. If I stay at the job, my income will steadily increase. It is impossible to find anything within decent commutable distance for less than 4k in that area that's not a complete run down shit hole or a 300sqft studio. I found a place for 4.6k that I really like but it will be 38% of my net pay (exc. utilities).

I know this goes against the usual PF advice, but I value my living space a lot and after working 70-80hrs the last thing I want is be depressed in some shitty apartment. On the other hand, I'm sacrificing so much of my time to give a ton of it away to the landlord. Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62078197
bump

62078200
>>62078160
if your commute is over 1.25 hours then just do it. ive been in ur same shoes didnhalf commute half live next to work and ten years later now i dont regret renting nearby.

commute absolutely kills your soul

62078205
well, it's $4.6k/month down the drain. only you can decide if it's worth it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62078208
God damn thats a lot. Just rent a shitbox. Burning 50k a year just for rent is killing your long term prosperity and will just delay retirement

62078211
Live in a shared 2 bedroom 1 bathroom house for less money. High rent eats your savings and commuting eats your time which is worse. If you are young then go for 4 roommates 1 bathroom and have fun partying with your roommates.
> t. lived in a shared 2 bed 1 bath house for years until roommate got tired of listening to me jerkin it and started slamming doors, then i moved out to a ~400sf studio

62078215
What job / industry?

62078265
>>62078160
Seems to be a lot of room between supreme luxury penthouse and shitbox. Landlords are generally amenable to you fixing things up so just focus on the area. The neighbors and that.

62078277
>working 70-80hrs
You're a wagie who will barely be home except to sleep. Just rent a cheap room to sleep in.

62078295
>>62078160
SF? NYC? The price tag is worth it if you have both leisure time and disposable income to enjoy them.

Considering the “70-80” hour work weeks, that’s unsustainable. If you do that more than once a year, you’ll eventually want to throw yourself off the balcony of the nicest penthouse $12k could afford. Your goal in life with a salary is to work as little as possible.

Yes, any commute is terrible. If you’re taking public transit in those two cities, it’s doable to live in an affordable area. I credit great public transit for giving me time to study and let my mind wander early in my career as a tech professional. An extra hour in your work day isn’t going to be worse than having no savings.

Here’s my warning - coastal high pressure industries like tech, law, and finance are churn and burn. They promise dreams, drain the youth and vitality, then kick the once-eager husks to the curb once a pulse of resistance is felt. I have seen many people fall from grace and unsuccessfully cope with drugs, women, flashy cars, nice apartments, and all of those markers of a successful life. Save money to prepare for layoffs and your next move. Don’t be a sucker and spend your life in a VHCOL without getting all you can.

62078404
>>62078160
Retard.

62078728
My counter to all of this is what's the point of making money if you aren't spending it? Sure, don't be a dumbfuck who doesn't save anything and wastes money but having a good place to live is not wasting money for me. There's also a social capital element of having a nice place where you can invite people. I know I will be successful and make more money and I feel like if anything spending more here will encourage me to find additional ways to make more.

62078770
>>62078160
If you find a partner who isn't a deadbeat then she could potentially pay half of the rent. Have that thought ever crossed your mind? If she is a catch maybe you will have to settle for paying 70% of the rent, still a major discount plus a wet hole bonus.

62078873
>>62078770
in the current stage of my life i'd rather fuck foids before I move to a city where most women don't have fucked up liberal values

62079016
>>62078873
Why do you care about their values if they pay rent and give access to their wet hole? The only problems are minor stuff like snoring and sharing a bed in general, and having to occasionally wait on her when you need the bathroom. Just find one that doesn't do any drugs, including alcohol and nicotine. There are plenty of these.

62079032
>>62078160
If youre White you need to leave the city immediately. If youre not, then I guess just continue enjoying your destruction of Western civilization while it still lasts. This is financial advice

62079040
>>62078295
^ Best advice in the thread.

62079171
>>62079032
I love advice like this. Yes I'm white and yes I fucking hate what's happening to the Western world. I'm extremely racist and hate the modern way of living. That said - tell me the other option? Move to Asia to live with peasants? Nah I'm good. I'll make my money and eventually retire to a ranch. For now this is a necessary evil. I fucking hate it sure, I wish our society wasn't as destroyed as it is but alas.

62079183
>>62079040
This >>62078404 is the best advice in this thread.

More like the CIA astroturfed and supplied weapons

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62079146

62079151
>>62079101

Here's the thing though - is there anyone left to fall for it for what, the third time now? There probably won't be any pumps on monday there way there were no dumps this weekend because everyone expects the orange buffoon to continue kicking the can down the road

62079153
>>62079146
this is all so schizophrenic
and probably intentionally schizophrenic

62079172
>>62079151
Doesnt matter if anyone falls for it. Trump is gonna taco then everyone will buy because they think everyone else will buy. The S&P will close green on monday

62079215
aaaaaaaaaa

62079259
>>62079101
*takes bibis oil in his mouth

62079276
>>62079101
That's how he keeps doing it: making up something really scary that's going to happen that is purely imaginary. Then announce that the threat of the really scary thing is over (actually it never existed), and everyone feels joyful and exuberant again.

item.title: Pulsar Helium — 12 replies — 32.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Pulsar Helium

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:24 UTC

OP

>What is it?

Pulsar helium is an emerging helium exploratory and development company. It offers a pure play on helium by focusing on helium extraction as a primary product, rather than a by product of natural gas extraction.

It currently has two emerging projects
https://pulsarhelium.com/projects/topaz/default.aspx
The first is it's Topaz project in Minnesota. Seven wells were recently drilled, and all seven reported significant helium concentrations. Jetstream 1 averaged 8.1% helium (with concentrations in some wells reaching 14%) and Jetstream 2 averaged 5.6%
It should be noted economically viable helium reservoirs only need to have a concentration of 0.3%. This makes the Topaz region the 2nd largest helium reserve in North America. The wells also contain an average of 71% CO2, giving the project the capability of producing both critical gasses currently facing shortages. Bipartisan support in Minnesota surrounding helium development will ensure the speedy development of this stake.

The 2nd is it's Tunu Project in greenland.
https://pulsarhelium.com/projects/tunu/default.aspx
While not as large as it's project in the Topaz region, the project boasts a well concentration of 0.8%, making it one of the largest primary reserves of Helium in all of Europe.

In both provinces Pulsar Helium holds First Mover status and EXCLUSIVE mineral exploration licenses. This makes Pulsar Helium the most lucrative pure play on helium 99% of you are missing.

>what's the chart like
Pulsar Helium (Ticker PLSR) is currently trading at $1.31/ share
It has a 52 week low of 0.29
And a high of 1.93. Last week saw it reach it's ATH, with its CEO taking advantage of and selling ~500k worth of shares.
There are currently 162 million shares, giving the company a Market Cap of 207m

Once development begins, Pulsar will grow into one of the largest helium producers in North America.

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=EHGkYuAePJw

Early replies (first 3)

62075797
>>62075780
ty fren

62075804
>>62075780
interesting. need to replace that precious Qatari He+ from somewhere

62075817
>>62075804
Yes, they produced around 1/3 of the world's helium, recent strikes by Iran have destroyed reportedly over 25% of their production capabilities for the foreseeable future. This has reduced the global helium output by 6.66%.
Further blockades of the hormussy will lead to increased shortages, making a domestic supply of helium an issue of national security.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62075823
>>62075817
whats helium do anyway?

62075856
>>62075823
Helium is an inert, non renewable gas critical to many industries.

Healthcare accounts for 40% of the world's usage, primarily in cooling the superconducting magnets in MRI machines
Welders use it as a shielding gas
Critical to semiconductor production
Can't make fiber optic cable without it
NASA uses it for rocket shit
The airbag in your car likely uses it
Cryogenics? Yeah that needs helium too

62075871
>>62075856
i wanted air ships so bad

62075872
>>62075823
>>62075856
and lab equipment for all kinds of analyses. Mining and resource exploration industries will come to a screaming halt without lab results.

62075899
If the CEO was so confident in his company, he should be buying shares not selling them into the price spike for exit liquidity.

If anything they should have used ATM dilution during the spike to increase funding for all the shit they need to process the helium

62075929
>>62075899
My mistake, it wasn't the CEO
>Pulsar Helium Inc. has disclosed that ABCrescent Cooperatief U.A., an entity associated with former director Brice Laurent, has sold 280,000 common shares of the company. Following this transaction, ABCrescent controls or directs 19,789,167 Pulsar shares, representing 10.68% of the company’s issued and outstanding share capital.
For further reading see https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/pulsar-helium-discloses-share-sale-by-former-directors-associated-entity
>>62075871
Airship would be KINO, but with CERN using it too cool their particle acceleratirs and chuds getting MRIs they will always be expensive and not for the plebs

62076558
>>62075823
Every MRI (hospital) and NMR (commercial and academic labs) instrument requires helium to operate. They can NOT go without regular helium top ups without risking the superconductors inside becoming literal bombs from the rapid electron resistance heat runaway.

62077527
>>62075780
OP here, about to drop an llm summary of my Tungsten pick

While this is not financial advice and involves significant speculation/high risk (as with all junior mining equities), here's a reasoned estimate of TUNGF's potential market cap if American Tungsten Corp. successfully reaches steady-state production at its targeted ~8% of U.S. tungsten demand level. This assumes they execute on the IMA Mine restart (500 tpd mill, ~1,825 tonnes WO3 equivalent per year from tungsten + moly/silver by-products), with no major delays, dilution beyond current plans, or execution failures.

>Production target: Company guidance is ~1,825 t WO3/year (≈182,500 MTU), equating to their stated ~8% of U.S. demand (plus ~10% U.S. moly and >300k oz silver credits).

>Gross revenue potential: Tungsten alone ~$500–550M annually at current prices (182,500 MTU × $2,900). By-products add material upside. (Note: Earlier pre-surge estimates were $80–150M; prices have transformed the economics

>Realistic central range: $1.0B – $3.0B USD (roughly 10–30x from current levels).

With china curbing exports, Tungsten America (Ticker TUNGF) is the obvious choice for a pure tungsten play. Timeline to restart the previous operational Mine (with EXCELLENT facilities still exisiting) is mid 2026 - Early 2027

62079199
>>62075823
Needed for MRIs etc, limited supply

item.title: UPDATE — 8 replies — 31.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:30 UTC

OP

What stocks have you been buying lately?

Today is a sad day for me, but especially my mother. After attempting to lessen the pain my cat is experiencing (she has terminal cancer), after 2 weeks of giving her appetite stimulants to help her eat and 2 days of steroids to help clear her lungs of fluids, we've decided it's finally time to euthanize her. She's slowly wasted away over the last month, going from 15 pounds to about 8 pounds now.

RIP.

Early replies (first 3)

62076720
She's gone now.

Burying her later today.

62076826
>>62076720
Sorry about your cat

62076838
>>62076826
Thank you Anon.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077282
The burial is done.

62079159
>>62076577
Nuclear ETFs and forget about it.
Otherwise, anything hardware related. Forget the "bubble talk" everything is going exactly as people thought months ago: companies are switching to on-prem infrastructure. They want local computer. Right now it's the companies that pay attention. Next year it'll be your retarded mom and pop places. Sidenote, if you need RAM, buy it now. It's not going down.
RIP cat

62079206
>>62076577
Dcaing into chinese tech ets.
Holding a bunch of defensives.
Sill holding gold
But i am mostly on HYSA, and bond Etfs

62079209
>>62079206
*chinese tech Etfs

62079228
>>62076577
TQQQ

item.title: NFT bros... — 11 replies — 20.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
NFT bros...

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:13 UTC

OP

NFT bros...

Early replies (first 3)

62076986
He didn't buy it

62076987
Paying $1.3 million for an ugly monkey jpg is one of those most retarded things ever

62076990
>>62076986
Ah this is far more likely, they probably paid him 1.3 million just to act like he had anything to do with it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62077015
>>62076986
Yeah it was part of a marketing team who paid celebs to say they bought it and gave them the nft

62077207
>>62077010
They wish they looked like that.

62078077
>>62076978
the bored ape looks heartbroken that its value dropped so much, kek

62078877
>>62076978
I see he still has that droopy vaxtard face

62079133
>>62076978
You guys are so dumb. He was approached like many others by the team that issued the NFTs. He was given one free and clear. He then bought the NFT from himself for $1.3 million. His 1.3 million is now in a new wallet that he controls. He likely traded this money for more crypto, to diversify. It was all a tax evasion scheme. Unfortunately for most of these clowns the market tanked and trapped them in losses they can't realize. Except bieber could sell his $15000 nft and claim a loss if he has any investment income to offset.

62079155
>>62079133
who the fuck is still buying NFTs

62079156
>>62076978
NFTs will come back btw

item.title: Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:37 UTC

OP

Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and dca into MSTR?

Early replies (first 3)

62079019
Don't ever touch your 401k retard thats fake money that you dont have.

62079034
>>62079012
>Is it worth it to pay the penalty to cash out my 401k and [do something else with it]
Almost never. Unless you've got some insanely retarded debt with ultra high interest rates or a life or death medical emergency, you have to leave it in the boomerk. It's frustrating, but think of it as the cost of the free money you got from your company's match.

62079038
>>62079012
>MSTR
Cash it out and go all in on 100% physical silver.
This is financial advice.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62079196
>>62079012
>Am I going to be a pussy faggot or take a chance and retire the next 10 generations of my bloodline?
MSTR is going to $4k at least, this is clown world after all so BTC at 1 million and sailor hailed as a genius is the only outcome that makes sense

62079251
>>62079038
I have some, but honestly a little worried about being able to sell without a 20% fee.
>>62079196
When do you think is a good idea to sell? I'm thinking to sell half at a 2x just to ride guarantee money or start to dca out after a 5x

item.title: Is Bridge through Wormhole a legit way to hold coins in MetaMask? — 3 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is Bridge through Wormhole a legit way to hold coins in MetaMask?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-05 15:28 UTC

OP

I want to own some Hyperliquid HYPE in my Metamask. Copying the contract id for the token on Uni swap and querying it in MetaMask, I see I can swap to it, but it's a "Wormhole" version of the coin. How legit is it? If I buy this version am I going to find the value destroyed years from now while HYPE is still going strong? I don't know anything about this shit

Early replies (first 3)

62079145
it's an iou I guess. For longterm holding you should swap it for hype on hyperevm with the wormhole bridge thing

62079177
>>62079037
Change now dot io and you send another gryfto and they swap with hype and send it to another wallet of your choice. I swap sol for bnb all the time

62079216
>>62079037
>>62079145
>>62079177
I'm and moran. I finally found the way to swap to native HYPE on hyperevm in meta. I now have big hype stacks both in meta and in my brokerage (through a swedish etc contract)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.
